fields.description:
## Context
The /dso:architect-foundation skill is recommended after onboarding, particularly for non-technical users directed to use --auto mode. Currently --auto skips the initial Socratic dialogue but still pauses three times: to ask whether the generated blueprint meets needs, to request acceptance of enforcement recommendations, and to confirm architectural invariants. These confirmation gates defeat the purpose of --auto for automated and non-technical onboarding flows.

## Success Criteria
- /dso:architect-foundation --auto runs from invocation to completion without any user prompts — verified by invoking the skill in a scripted test environment (fixture project) and asserting: (1) no stdin reads occur, (2) ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md is produced, (3) at least one ADR file is written to docs/adr/. No blueprint approval gate, no enforcement recommendation acceptance, no architectural invariant confirmation.
- In --auto mode, all decisions that would normally require confirmation use recommended defaults; the skill's output documents the choices made so a user can inspect results afterward.
- Artifacts generated by --auto (ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md, docs/adr/) are identical in structure and completeness to those produced by interactive mode when the user accepts all recommended defaults.

## Dependencies
Coordination dependency for the onboarding remediation epic — this epic should complete before or alongside it.

## Approach
Remove the three confirmation gates from the --auto code path in architect-foundation/SKILL.md. Replace each gate with automatic selection of the recommended default and a logged summary of the choice made.

## Scenario Analysis
- Silent default selection masks incompatible project structure (high, runtime): Blueprint misidentification silently propagates — wrong enforcement rules written with no correction point.
- Recommended defaults conflict with existing dso-config.conf values (medium, configuration): Auto-selected enforcement defaults may contradict deliberate team customizations without surfacing a conflict warning.

## Done Definitions

- When complete, /dso:architect-foundation --auto runs from invocation to completion without any user prompts on a fixture project, verified by: (1) no stdin reads occur, (2) ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md is produced, (3) at least one ADR file is written to docs/adr/
  <- Satisfies: "/dso:architect-foundation --auto runs from invocation to completion without any user prompts"
- When complete, all three former confirmation gates in --auto mode are replaced with automatic selection of the recommended default, and the skill's output log documents each choice made so a user can inspect results afterward
  <- Satisfies: "all decisions that would normally require confirmation use recommended defaults; the skill's output documents the choices made"
- When complete, ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md and docs/adr/ artifacts produced by --auto are structurally identical to those produced by interactive mode when the user accepts all recommended defaults, verified by running both modes on the same fixture project and diffing outputs
  <- Satisfies: "Artifacts generated by --auto are identical in structure and completeness to those produced by interactive mode when the user accepts all recommended defaults"

## Scope
- IN: Identify and remove the 3 confirmation gates from --auto code path in architect-foundation/SKILL.md; add logged summary for each auto-selected default; verify no new gates are introduced
- OUT: Interactive mode behavior (unchanged), non-auto code path changes, ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md content changes

## Considerations
- [Reliability] Silent default selection scenario — auto-selected defaults must check existing dso-config.conf values and surface conflicts in the output log rather than silently overwriting
- [Testing] Scripted fixture must verify "no stdin reads" — redirect stdin from /dev/null during test run and verify exit 0
- [Maintainability] SKILL.md is a large LLM prompt file — the 3 gate locations must be precisely identified and annotated before modification to avoid breaking surrounding context
